agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Serum ferritin is the most sensitive test for detecting iron depletion in pregnancy because it directly reflects stored iron and decreases before clinically significant changes occur. It surpasses other markers such as serum iron, transferrin, or total iron-binding capacity due to its ability to identify early deficiencies prior to hematological alterations. \boxed{B}

The summary above contains redundant content from multiple sources; please revise only what was necessary while preserving all essential information within a tight constraint—no more than two concise sentences—and finalize with $\boxed{\text{letter}}$. 

Final Summary:

Serum ferritin is the most sensitive test for detecting iron depletion in pregnancy because it directly indicates available iron stores and diminishes first in deficiency. Though inflamed tissues may elevate ferritin falsely, it remains preferable to serum iron, transferrin, or TIBC for timely diagnosis of early iron insufficiency. \boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
